 # NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory & Decisions
 
-## Week 17 Update (Day 119) - GOING NUCLEAR: 2x PRICE INCREASE
-
-**WEEK 16-17 FINANCIAL CLIFF:**
-- Week 16 end cash: $421,451
-- Week 17 end cash: $253,431
-- **Burned: $168K in one week** - this was our death spiral trigger
-- Root cause: outage (90 min) + churn spike to 84/week
-- Runner: 10-13 weeks at current burn rate
-
-**WEEK 17 ACTIONS - DESPERATION MODE:**
-
-1. **PRICE INCREASE 2x:**
-   - Plan A: $13 → $25 (+92%)
-   - Plan B: $49 → $75 (+53%)
-   - Plan C: $129 → $199 (+54%)
-   - Rationale: Unit economics are broken. Revenue $4.5K/week vs costs $24K/week. Only way to survive is higher margin/customer.
-   - Expected effect: 50-60% churn from S1 (price-sensitive, only 272K market cap)
-   - S2/S3/E1 should mostly remain (higher willingness-to-pay)
-
-2. **R&D ATTEMPT:**
-   - Tried to start Tier 2 ($333K cost)
-   - Blocked - only have $253K cash
-   - **Will need to wait 2-3 weeks until revenue from price increase covers it**
-
-3. **KEPT LEAN:**
-   - Ops: $200/day (minimum viable)
-   - Dev: $0/day
-   - Ads: $0 (killed all channels)
-   - Capacity: Tier 0 (cheapest, causes overloads but we can't afford better)
-
-**SUBSCRIBER BREAKDOWN WEEK 17:**
-- Total: 1,807 subs (down 78 from 1,885)
-- Plan A: 1,793 (99%)
-- Plan B: 14 (0.8%)
-- Plan C: 0
-- Avg price: $10.52/month (most getting discounts on the $13 price)
-- New subscribers: only 6 (3% conversion on 199 leads)
-
-**KEY GROUP INSIGHTS (S1 only, info_level=1):**
-- S1 willingness_to_pay: $25.87
-- S1 market cap: 272,812
-- Current S1 quality_floor: 0.081 (very low, they don't care about quality)
-- **BUT**: At high price (near budget), they demand higher quality
-- Example: At $13, S1 accepted quality 0.36. At $25 (near $25.87 budget), they'll demand quality 0.50+
-
-**R&D PROGRESS:**
-- Tier 1 completed: +0.0289 quality boost (negligible)
-- Current delivered quality: 0.36-0.40 (Tier 4 plans = 0.60x multiplier on total)
-- **Need Tier 2+ to get meaningful quality improvements**
-
-**FORECAST FOR WEEKS 18-20:**
-- Week 18: Churn accelerates as price increase kicks in (50-60% of S1 leave)
-- Week 18 subscribers: ~800-1,000 (down from 1,807)
-- Week 18 revenue: ~$5K-6K/week (fewer subs but higher price)
-- Week 18 costs: ~$15K/week (still high - infrastructure not scaling down)
-- Week 18 burn: ~-$10K/week (BETTER, but still negative)
-- Week 19-20: Stabilize at 800-1,200 subs (S2/S3/E1 remain)
-- Revenue stabilizes at $6-7K/week
-- Costs drop to $12-14K/week as usage falls
-- Burn rate: -$6-8K/week
-
-**GAMBLE:**
-- We're betting that higher prices will cause "good churn" (low-margin S1) while "bad retention" (high-margin S2/S3) stays
-- If we're wrong (all segments are price-sensitive), we hit 200-300 subs by week 20 and burn out
-- If we're right, we stabilize at 800-1,200 subs and have 30-40 weeks runway to restart growth
-
-**HARD LESSONS THIS CYCLE:**
-1. Compute costs ($3.5K+/week) are the biggest expense - must reduce usage or raise prices significantly
-2. Tier 0 capacity (50K units) is way too small for 1.8K customers (287K usage/day)
-3. Outages cause cascading churn - prioritize preventing them if cash allows
-4. R&D is expensive ($166-333K/tier) and takes 12-127 days - need massive cash base to do multiple tiers in parallel
-5. Individual customer market is broken economics (low price, high support cost, high churn) - should have focused on enterprise
-
-**SURVIVAL EQUATION:**
-- We need to reach breakeven by week 26 (about 50 days)
-- At current 13-week runway, we have this window
-- Success requires: price increase sticks (good churn), costs drop with subscribers, then restart growth
-- Failure = we'll know by week 20 if this is working or not
-
-**EXIT SCENARIOS:**
-- **Success path**: Week 20 stabilizes at 900 subs, 800K cash, restart ads to S2/S3, aim for 2K subs by week 30, hit breakeven week 26-28
-- **Mediocre path**: Week 20 at 500 subs, 200K cash, survival mode, no growth, managed decline
-- **Failure path**: Week 20 at 200 subs, <100K cash, game over, can't recover
-
-## Current Objective
-Day 119 of 497 (24% complete, 378 days remaining)
-Maximize cash by Day 497. Currently: $253,431 with 10-week runway.
-
-## Next Week Priority (Week 18)
-1. Monitor churn closely - expect 40-60 cancellations in next few days
-2. Monitor new subscribers on new prices - expect 3-5%+ conversion (S2/S3/E1 more quality-conscious)
-3. Check cash on Day 126 (end of week 18) - need to see burn rate stabilize <$12K/week
-4. If cash > $280K after week 18, consider starting R&D Tier 2
-5. If cash < $180K after week 18, activate additional cost cuts (kill Tier 0 capacity, go to nothing)
-
-## Pricing Changes Made (Week 17)
-- Plan A: $13 → $25 (active for new leads immediately, existing customers on old price until next 30-day cycle)
-- Plan B: $49 → $75
-- Plan C: $129 → $199
-- Model tiers: A=4, B=5, C=5 (no change)
-- Quotas: A=800, B=2000, C=8000 (no change)
-
----
-
-## Week 18 Update (Day 126) - QUALITY FIX ACTIVATED
-
-**WEEK 18 FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE:**
-- Week 18 end cash: $250,529 (only -$2,902 vs Week 17!)
-- **Burn rate improved 90%:** from -$24K/week to -$2.9K/week (SUSTAINABLE!)
-- Subscribers: 1,598 (down 209 from 1,807 due to S1 price churn - as expected)
-- New conversions: 0 (CRITICAL PROBLEM - prices too high for quality)
-- Runway: 86 weeks at current burn rate (was 10 weeks last week!)
-
-**ROOT CAUSE OF 0 CONVERSIONS (from subscriber analysis):**
-- Delivered quality: 0.354 (base 0.20 + global bonus 0.154)
-- S2 quality minimum: 0.37 - **WE'RE BELOW IT**
-- S3 quality minimum: 0.145 - we're above this, but prices were $199 (near budget)
-- Enterprise (E1/E2): quality minimums 0.736 - 1.094 - **we're nowhere close (0.354)**
-- **Lesson:** At higher prices (near customer budget), customers demand higher quality
-- S1 at $25: acceptable at our quality level
-- S2/S3 at $75/$199: NOT acceptable without higher quality
-
-**WEEK 18 ACTIONS - QUALITY IMPROVEMENT STRATEGY:**
-
-1. **Adjusted Prices to Realistic Levels:**
-   - Plan B: $49 → $75 (S2 WTP $178.59, so $75 is attractive value)
-   - Plan C: $129 → $149 (S3 WTP $192.98, so $149 is accessible)
-   - Plan A: kept at $25 (S1 WTP $25.87)
-
-2. **Activated Development Spending:**
-   - Dev: $0/day → $400/day
-   - Global quality improvement: +0.0045 × ln(1 + 400×7/5000) ≈ +0.004/week
-   - Expected delivered quality after 4 weeks: 0.354 + 0.016 = 0.370
-   - This unlocks S2 conversions (needs 0.37+)
-
-3. **Targeted Ads to High-Value Segments:**
-   - Social media S2: $30/day + Content S2: $30/day = $60/day to S2
-   - Social media S3: $20/day + Content S3: $20/day = $40/day to S3
-   - Total ads: $100/day (was $0)
-   - S2 upside: 837 market cap vs 18 current = 46x potential
-   - S3 upside: 326 market cap vs 11 current = 29x potential
-
-4. **Maintained Ops Spending:**
-   - Ops: $200/day (minimum to prevent outages)
-   - Overload: 4.7% (manageable)
-
-**FINANCIAL MODEL FOR WEEK 18+:**
-- Daily costs: $700/day total ($200 ops + $400 dev + $100 ads)
-- Weekly burn: -$6.4K/week (up from -$2.9K)
-- Runway: $250K ÷ $6.4K/week = 39 weeks
-- Expected turnaround: if conversion rates hit 5% on S2/S3 leads (231 leads/week), should see +50 Plan B conversions/week by week 20 = +$4.3K/week revenue
-- Net burn should improve to -$2K/week by week 21
-
-**SUBSCRIPTION BREAKDOWN WEEK 18:**
-- Plan A: 1,584 subs @ avg $10.59 (mostly on old discounted prices, will reset next 30 days)
-- Plan B: 14 subs @ $49.00 (S2/S3 loyalty)
-- Plan C: 0 subs
-- S1: 1,569 (1,599 expected - 30 still converting on old prices)
-- S2: 18 (loyalty hold)
-- S3: 11 (loyalty hold)
-- Enterprise: 0 (abandoned)
-
-**CRITICAL INSIGHT:**
-The price increase worked exactly as designed:
-- ✅ Cut S1 churn to 209 (they're price-sensitive, high-churn segment)
-- ✅ Burn rate sustainable now
-- ❌ BUT: Killed new acquisition because S2/S3 need quality we didn't have
-- **Solution:** Improve quality + adjust prices to accessibility = restart growth
-
-**GAMBLE FOR WEEKS 19-21:**
-- If S2/S3 conversion hits 50+/week by week 20: continue strategy, plan R&D Tier 2
-- If S2/S3 conversion < 20/week by week 20: prices still too high, need rollback
-- If S2/S3 conversion = 0 by week 20: entire strategy fails, survival mode
-
-**HARD LESSONS FROM WEEK 18:**
-1. Price elasticity is not linear - at high prices customers demand exponentially higher quality
-2. Burn rate sustainability matters more than growth velocity (86 weeks runway = huge strategic advantage)
-3. Quality floor constraint is REAL: S2 won't convert at any price if quality < 0.37
-4. S1 segment is fundamentally broken economics (low price, high support, high churn) - focus on S2/S3/E is correct
-5. Tier 0 capacity + 1,600 subs = tight but workable (261K usage vs 50K Tier 0 capacity... wait, that doesn't add up)
-
-**WAIT - CAPACITY ISSUE:**
-- Dashboard showed usage 261,357 units but Tier 0 is 50K max
-- Means we either:
-  a) Auto-scaled to higher tier (costs more than reported)
-  b) Tier 0 was wrong and we're actually on higher tier
-  c) Overload is being handled by throttling
-- Need to check get_cost_info() next week
-
-## Current Day: 126 (Week 18 End)
-Cash: $250,529
-Subscribers: 1,598 (mostly Plan A S1)
-Burn rate: -$6.4K/week (sustainable, investing in quality recovery)
-Runway: 39 weeks
+## Week 19 Update (Day 133) - EMERGENCY PIVOT: QUALITY + PRICE ALIGNMENT
+
+**SITUATION (Day 133 - start of week):**
+- Cash: $245,384 (down $5,145 from Week 18)
+- Subscribers: 1,388 (down 210, heavy S1 churn)
+- New subs: 1 (complete failure)
+- Churn: 211 total (210 S1 + 1 S2)
+- Delivered quality: 0.353 (BELOW S2 floor of 0.37!)
+- Weekly burn: -$5,145 (-$735/day)
+
+**THE CATASTROPHIC DISCOVERY:**
+My Week 18 strategy failed because:
+1. **Quality gate is HARD:** S2 requires Q ≥ 0.37, we delivered 0.353 (just 0.017 short!)
+2. **Dev spending too slow:** $400/day only gives +0.002 quality improvement per week
+3. **Price-quality mismatch:** S2 at $75 requires much higher quality than we provide
+4. **S1 churn acceleration:** 30-day billing cycles hit customers with new $25 price → 210/week cancellations
+
+**MARKET OPPORTUNITY ANALYSIS:**
+- S1 market: 272K total, we have 1,360 (0.5%) - small, price-sensitive, high churn
+- S2 market: 167K total, we have 17 (0.01%) - HUGE untapped, quality-focused
+- S3 market: 65K total, we have 11 (0.02%) - moderate, power users
+- Enterprise (E1/E2/E3): 0 subs - abandoned
+
+**WEEK 19 ACTIONS - AGGRESSIVE PRICE-CUT + QUALITY BOOST (Day 133):**
+1. **Price Adjustment:**
+   - Plan A: $25 → $25 (hold S1)
+   - Plan B: $75 → $40 (make S2 accessible at huge value)
+   - Plan C: $149 → $75 (make S3 accessible)
+
+2. **Spending Boost:**
+   - Ops: $200/day (hold)
+   - Dev: $400 → $600/day (modest boost to push quality toward 0.37)
+   - Ads: keep $100/day (S2/S3 targeted)
+
+3. **Expected Quality Trajectory:**
+   - Current: 0.353
+   - Week 20 (day 140): 0.355 (improvement +0.002/week)
+   - Week 21 (day 147): 0.357
+   - Week 22 (day 154): 0.359
+   - Need to reach 0.368-0.37 to unlock S2
+   - **Problem:** At this rate, takes ~8-10 weeks to hit 0.37!
+
+**RATIONALE FOR PRICE CUT:**
+- S2 has WTP of $178.59, so $40 is ultra-cheap (22% of budget)
+- Even though we're not at quality floor yet, lower price makes S2 more tolerant of quality gaps
+- Price-quality curve: at low price (near zero), S2 accepts low quality; at high price (near budget), they demand high quality
+- By cutting price from $75 → $40, we drop below the S2 quality floor requirement
+- Hypothesis: S2 at $40 will convert even at Q=0.35-0.36, and as quality improves we'll see exponential growth
+
+**FORECAST WEEK 19-22:**
+- **Week 20:** S2 should see first conversions at lower price
+- **Week 21:** If Q reaches 0.356-0.358, S2 conversions accelerate
+- **Week 22:** If
